samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>A simplified radix-2 divider for 8-bit signed or unsigned integers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">The clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst">The reset signal to initialize or reset the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">Indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">The 8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">The 8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">A 1-bit input signal indicating that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">A 1-bit input signal indicating that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">A 1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">The 16-bit output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="DIVIDEND_REG">Stores the dividend during division operations.</constant>
        <constant id="DIVISOR_REG">Stores the divisor during division operations.</constant>
        <constant id="NEG_DIVISOR">Stores the negative value of the divisor.</constant>
        <constant id="SR">Shift register used for storing the remainder and quotient.</constant>
        <constant id="CNT">Counter used to keep track of the number of iterations.</constant>
        <constant id="START_CNT">Flag indicating the start of the division process.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="DIVIDEND_REG" type="internal"/>
        <reg id="DIVISOR_REG" type="internal"/>
        <reg id="NEG_DIVISOR" type="internal"/>
        <reg id="SR" type="internal" width_description="[15:0]"/>
        <reg id="CNT" type="internal" width_description="[3:0]"/>
        <reg id="START_CNT" type="internal"/>
        
        <always @(posedge clk or posedge rst)>
            if(rst) begin
                DIVIDEND_REG <= 8'd0;
                DIVISOR_REG <= 8'd0;
                NEG_DIVISOR <= 8'd0;
                SR <= 16'd0;
                CNT <= 4'd0;
                START_CNT <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                if(opn_valid && !res_valid) begin
                    DIVIDEND_REG <= dividend;
                    DIVISOR_REG <= divisor;
                    NEG_DIVISOR <= ~divisor + 1;
                    SR[15:8] <= 8'd0;
                    SR[7:0] <= {dividend[7], dividend};
                    CNT <= 4'd1;
                    START_CNT <= 1'b1;
                end else if(start_cnt) begin
                    if(CNT[3]) begin
                        CNT <= 4'd0;
                        START_CNT <= 1'b0;
                        SR[15:8] <= SR[15:8];
                        SR[7:0] <= {SR[15:8][7], SR[15:8]};
                    end else begin
                        CNT <= CNT + 1;
                        SR[15:8] <= SR[15:8];
                        SR[7:0] <= {SR[15:8][7], SR[15:8]};
                    end
                end
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Division operation starts when opn_valid is high and res_valid is low
        if (opn_valid && !res_valid) begin
            // Save the inputs
            DIVIDEND_REG = dividend;
            DIVISOR_REG = divisor;
            NEG_DIVISOR = ~divisor + 1;
            
            // Initialize the shift register SR with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit
            SR = {{8{DIVIDEND_REG[7]}}, DIVIDEND_REG} << 1;
            
            // Set NEG_DIVISOR to the negated absolute value of the divisor
            NEG_DIVISOR = ~DIVISOR_REG + 1;
            
            // Set the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process
            CNT = 1;
            START_CNT = 1;
        end
        
        // If start_cnt is high, perform the division process
        if (start_cnt) begin
            // If the counter cnt reaches 8 (most significant bit of cnt is set), the division is complete
            if (CNT[3]) begin
                // Clear the counter cnt and start_cnt
                CNT = 0;
                START_CNT = 0;
                
                // Update the shift register SR with the final remainder and quotient
                SR = {SR[15:8], SR[7:0]};
            end else begin
                // Increment the counter cnt
                CNT = CNT + 1;
                
                // Perform the subtraction operation
                SUBTRACT_RESULT = SR[15:8] + NEG_DIVISOR;
                
                // Use a multiplexer to select the appropriate result based on the carry-out
                if (SUBTRACT_RESULT[8]) begin
                    SR[15:8] = SUBTRACT_RESULT[7:0];
                    SR[7:0] = {SR[15:8][7], SR[15:8]};
                end else begin
                    SR[15:8] = SUBTRACT_RESULT[7:0];
                    SR[7:0] = {SR[15:8][7], SR[15:8]};
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
    
    <task id="reset_task">
        <description>Resets the module's internal state.</description>
        <sequence>
            <step>Set DIVIDEND_REG to 8'd0.</step>
            <step>Set DIVISOR_REG to 8'd0.</step>
            <step>Set NEG_DIVISOR to 8'd0.</step>
            <step>Set SR to 16'd0.</step>
            <step>Set CNT to 4'd0.</step>
            <step>Set START_CNT to 1'b0.</step>
        </sequence>
    </task>
</module>
